Details: Effect of chloramines on the oxidation/ removal of arsenic was studied in two bench-scale experiments. One study used formulated challenge water similar in composition to surface and ground waters. In that case, a monochloramine dose of 0.10 milligrams per liter was added to the oxidation reactor. A 40 percent oxidation of As(III) to As(V) was observed. In the other study, a real surface water sample was used, and a high chloramine residual was obtained. Both studies indicated that chloramines are not very effective in the conversion from As(III) to As(V) [172, 607].
